methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official Graduate Research Handbook conduct guidance, an authorship procedure, a graduate research PDF, and multiple course catalogue pages. Course-only results were not used for policy claims.
Firecrawl search found the official Academic Integrity Policy, Academic Misconduct Procedure, Assessment Procedure, Cyber Security Policy, and a generic student technology landing page.
Firecrawl search found the official Online exam rules page. A schools workshop page and a degree page were not used because they were event/course/admissions specific.
Firecrawl search found the official Technology landing page, Online exam rules, graduate research handbook pages, and course catalogue pages.

The Technology landing page linked to an Artificial Intelligence tools route. The public adelaideuni.edu.au/adelaide.edu.au route returned a 404, while the students.adelaide.edu.au route redirected to an Okta sign-in flow.
No public alternate Artificial Intelligence tools page was found on the students subdomain.

artifacts.summary: The crawl found official Adelaide University sources for academic-integrity treatment of inappropriate AI use, misconduct outcomes, coursework assessment communication about generative AI, online-exam AI-tool restrictions, graduate-research AI use and data-handling limits, research authorship disclosure, and cyber-security handling of AI systems. Official transition-era URLs on adelaideuni.edu.au redirected to adelaide.edu.au for fetched content; claims are scoped to those official Adelaide University pages.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic Integrity Policy | Policies and Procedures | Adelaide University
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Adelaide University policy page, fetched through the adelaideuni.edu.au source URL and resolved to the institution's adelaide.edu.au policy URL. The page defines inappropriate AI use within academic misconduct examples.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic Misconduct Procedure | Policies and Procedures | Adelaide University
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Adelaide University procedure page. It states how academic misconduct is managed and includes outcome guidance for inappropriate use of artificial intelligence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Assessment Procedure | Policies and Procedures | Adelaide University
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Adelaide University assessment procedure. The page applies to coursework assessment and includes a specific requirement to inform students about appropriate generative AI use.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Online exam rules | Current Students | Adelaide University
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Adelaide University current-students page for invigilated online exam rules. It specifically lists no AI tools under approved-tools-only conditions.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Conduct of research | Graduate Research School | Adelaide University
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Adelaide University Graduate Research School handbook page with a dedicated Using generative AI section for graduate research students.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Authorship Procedure | Policies and Procedures | Adelaide University
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Adelaide University authorship procedure with specific generative-AI authorship and disclosure rules for research outputs.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Cyber Security Policy | Policies and Procedures | Adelaide University
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Adelaide University cyber security policy with a specific AI systems and data security principle.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Technology | Adelaide University
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official student technology landing page, but the page only links to Artificial Intelligence tools and does not itself contain AI policy or data-handling rules.

stopConditions: Stop after verified official policy/guidance pages with direct AI-specific evidence are captured.
Do not use course catalogue, outreach, generic landing, login-required, or 404 pages as claim evidence.
Do not bypass institutional sign-in, WAF, CAPTCHA, paywalls, or robots restrictions.

Route redirected to institutional Okta sign-in; no login bypass attempted.

artifacts.claimText: Adelaide University's Academic Integrity Policy treats inappropriate use of AI that subverts an assignment or gives unfair academic advantage as an example of academic misconduct, including submitting AI-produced work as the student's own or using AI-generated information without acknowledgement.

citation.citationTitle: Academic Integrity Policy | Policies and Procedures | Adelaide University
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Adelaide University's Academic Misconduct Procedure lists inappropriate use of Artificial Intelligence with plagiarism in its outcome table, with a standard outcome of mark reduction up to and including zero for the assignment.

citation.citationTitle: Academic Misconduct Procedure | Policies and Procedures | Adelaide University
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For Adelaide University coursework assessment, the Assessment Procedure states that course coordinators will ensure students are informed about appropriate use of generative AI and similar tools in the course.

artifacts.claimText: Adelaide University's invigilated online exam rules state that students may use only tools, software, or materials explicitly approved in exam instructions, and list AI tools such as ChatGPT as not permitted.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic Integrity Policy | Policies and Procedures | Adelaide University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Inappropriate use of artificial intelligence (AI) - is to subvert the aims of assignment or give the student an unfair academic advantage. Inappropriate use of AI can include but is not limited to: submitting work produced (or produced in part) by generative artificial intelligence as the student's own work; using information generated by artificial intelligence without acknowledgement or attribution.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Definitions used in our policy - Academic Misconduct
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Academic Integrity Policy | Policies and Procedures | Adelaide University
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic Misconduct Procedure | Policies and Procedures | Adelaide University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Type of academic misconduct: Plagiarism; Inappropriate use of Artificial Intelligence. Standard Outcome: Reduction of mark up to and including zero for the assignment. Mitigated outcome: a warning; submission of a corrected version of the affected component for a maximum of 50% for assignment; a reduction of mark. Exacerbated outcome: zero for assignment; zero for course.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Online exam rules | Current Students | Adelaide University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Note: Anything not listed is prohibited. Use only tools, software, or materials explicitly approved in your exam instructions. This includes: No collaboration tools (email, forums, etc.); No additional electronic devices; No AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT); No physical items (pens, paper, calculators).

artifacts.sourceTitle: Conduct of research | Graduate Research School | Adelaide University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Generative AI tools generate new content such as text or images based on prompts from the user. These tools can support your research, but their use must be transparent and responsible. You must: discuss AI use with your supervisor; keep records of programs or applications used, dates and prompts; include an AI use statement in your thesis acknowledgements; acknowledge AI use in presentations and publications.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Conduct of research | Graduate Research School | Adelaide University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: You must not: generate thesis content using AI (unless quoted and cited); translate large portions of text using AI; upload sensitive, copyrighted or confidential information into AI tools; upload unprotected intellectual property into AI tools.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Generative AI can be used in publications to improve the readability and clarity of written content by assisting authors with generating drafts, revising language, and suggesting phrasing. Generative AI must not be considered an author itself, and its use must be disclosed transparently.

artifacts.title: Adelaide University AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Adelaide University includes official transition-era Adelaide University policy and guidance pages covering inappropriate AI use in academic integrity, misconduct outcomes, coursework assessment communication, online exam restrictions, graduate research transparency and data-handling limits, authorship disclosure, and cyber-security treatment of AI systems.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Official adelaideuni.edu.au source URLs redirected to adelaide.edu.au final URLs during fetching; claims are scoped to the fetched Adelaide University pages.
The linked public Artificial Intelligence tools page was unavailable or login-gated, so no endorsed-tool list claim was staged.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
